lessons.trigger: Ran audit against cora — a project with pairmode scaffold but no pairmode_context.json
lessons.problem: 44 INCONSISTENT findings for every section body because fallback context renders all template variables as empty strings. The output is confusing — it looks like everything needs repair, when really the project just needs to be bootstrapped with pairmode to generate the context file.
lessons.learning: When audit detects no pairmode_context.json, it should emit a prominent warning: 'No pairmode_context.json found — template body comparison will show false INCONSISTENT for all variable-bearing sections. Bootstrap this project with /anchor:pairmode to fix.' MISSING and EXTRA findings remain reliable; INCONSISTENT findings require a context file to be meaningful.

methodology_change.description: Add a warning in audit output when pairmode_context.json is missing. Ideally suppress INCONSISTENT findings entirely when no context file exists, or clearly label them as 'body comparison unreliable — no context file'.

lessons.trigger: Ran audit against radar and forqsite — projects with pairmode scaffold
lessons.problem: INCONSISTENT findings include entries with section keys like '---' and '---__0'. These are separator lines (_split_sections treats '---' as a section delimiter and the text after it as the section body). Users see 'Section --- content differs from canonical template' and cannot tell what content is being compared or where to look in the file.
lessons.learning: The _split_sections output uses '---' as section keys in audit output. These entries are hard to interpret. Either: (a) skip separator-keyed sections from INCONSISTENT comparison since --- separators carry no semantic content, or (b) display the surrounding context (what section comes before/after) when reporting --- inconsistencies.

methodology_change.description: In audit.py _split_sections or the INCONSISTENT loop, skip or relabel entries whose section key starts with '---' to reduce noise in audit output.

lessons.trigger: Bootstrap dogfood on anchor — re-running after bug fixes
lessons.problem: Bootstrap overwrote hand-authored .claude/agents/ files with template output that was strictly worse: truncated checklist names, empty layer-rule sections, build_command interpolated into prose.
lessons.learning: For mature projects, templates are a starting point, not a replacement. Files that already exist should be skipped by default (same as CLAUDE.md/CLAUDE.build.md), or bootstrap should warn before overwriting hand-authored content.

methodology_change.description: Bootstrap should skip existing agent files by default. Add a --force-agents flag for intentional overwrites. Document in SKILL.md that agent files are treated as project-owned after first bootstrap.

lessons.trigger: Bootstrap dogfood — builder.md Python standards section
lessons.problem: builder.md template uses {{ build_command }} inside the 'All Python execution uses ...' prose, producing 'All Python execution uses PATH=$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH uv run pytest tests/pairmode/'. The build command is not the Python runtime.
lessons.learning: Template variables should be scoped to their semantic slot. build_command belongs in the build-gate section only; the Python standards section should hardcode 'uv run'.

methodology_change.description: Fix builder.md.j2: replace '{{ build_command }}' in the Python standards prose with the literal string 'uv run'. Use build_command only in the build-gate verification block.

lessons.trigger: Bootstrap dogfood — reviewer.md checklist from spec non-negotiables
lessons.problem: checklist_deriver produces items whose 'name' field is the first N chars of the non-negotiable text, truncated mid-sentence. reviewer.md became a 595-line wall of truncated constraint prose instead of a human-readable checklist.
lessons.learning: Non-negotiables are constraints, not checklist item names. The reviewer checklist needs human-authored label + short action question, not raw spec text. checklist_deriver should either produce module-scoped labels (e.g. 'companion-skill: spec write isolation') or not populate the reviewer checklist at all — leaving that to the human.

methodology_change.description: Remove spec-derived checklist_items from the reviewer.md template, or change the template to use module+ordinal labels. The reviewer checklist should be maintained by the developer, seeded with universal items only.

lessons.trigger: Dogfood audit run on anchor after clean bootstrap
lessons.problem: Audit reports INCONSISTENT for every hand-authored section that is richer than the template. It cannot distinguish 'unintentional drift' from 'intentional override with better content'. Result: a mature project sees constant audit noise it can never resolve without degrading its docs.
lessons.learning: Audit needs a way to mark sections as intentionally overridden. Without that signal, any project that customises its scaffold will permanently live in a noisy INCONSISTENT state, eroding trust in the tool.

methodology_change.description: Add a per-section override marker (e.g. a comment tag like '{# PROJECT_OVERRIDE #}') that audit treats as EXTRA rather than INCONSISTENT. Alternatively, add a .pairmode-overrides file listing section keys the project owns. Either approach lets audit surface real drift without nagging about deliberate customisation.
